title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: March 28, 2026: Stocks Fall for Fifth Straight Week Amid Geopolitical Tensions

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Mark Fifth Consecutive Losing Week as Geopolitical Tensions Escalate
items.summary: U.S. stocks deepened their drops on Friday, concluding a fifth straight week of losses, marking the longest such streak in nearly four years. Overall market sentiment remains cautious, primarily driven by ongoing tensions in the Middle East and the resulting surge in oil prices. Both the Dow Jones Industrial Average and the Nasdaq Composite have officially entered 'correction' territory, each down over 10% from their recent record highs. Consumer sentiment has also fallen significantly, with inflation concerns weighing heavily on the short-run economic outlook.

items.statContext: Fell 1.7%, closing its worst week since the Iran war began. It is 8.7% below its January all-time high.
Dropped 793.47 points, or 1.7%, and is down more than 10% from its record.
Sank 459.72 points, or 2.1%, now officially in 'correction' territory, down over 10% from its record.

items.headline: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings
items.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings declined sharply as consumers reduce spending on non-essential items due to higher gasoline prices.

items.headline: Starbucks
items.summary: Starbucks shares dropped amidst a broader sell-off in non-essential consumer goods.

items.headline: Chipotle Mexican Grill
items.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill saw its stock sink as discretionary spending by consumers tightened.

items.headline: Amazon
items.summary: Big Tech stocks, including Amazon, were among the heaviest weights on the market.

items.headline: Meta Platforms
items.summary: Meta Platforms contributed to the market's decline as Big Tech faced downward pressure.

items.headline: Energy and Basic Materials Lead, Tech and Communication Services Lag
items.summary: For the week ending March 27, the Energy sector emerged as the best performer, gaining 6.12%, followed by Basic Materials, up 4.23%. In contrast, Communication Services and Technology were the worst-performing sectors, declining 4.46% and 3.51% respectively. Growth stocks generally underperformed during this period of market turmoil.

items.headline: Fed Governor Discusses Balance Sheet Reduction
items.summary: Federal Reserve Board Governor Stephen Miran delivered a speech on the 'Prospects for Shrinking the Fed's Balance Sheet,' outlining reasons and steps for a gradual reduction to avoid market disruption. The Fed's balance sheet currently stands at 84% of nominal GDP.

items.headline: U.S. Consumer Sentiment Plunges in March Amid Iran Conflict and Rising Inflation
items.summary: U.S. consumer sentiment dropped 6% in March to its lowest level since December 2025, according to the University of Michigan. The decline is attributed to rising energy prices, volatile financial markets, and the ongoing conflict with Iran. Year-ahead inflation expectations rose from 3.4% in February to 3.8% in March, the largest one-month increase since April 2025.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Continue to Fuel Oil Prices and Market Volatility
items.summary: Ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, including US and Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear sites and Iran's continued hold on the Strait of Hormuz, have significantly impacted global markets. Brent crude climbed 4.2% to $112.57, and U.S. benchmark WTI jumped 5.5% to $99.64, driven by fears of disrupted oil supplies and global inflation. President Trump's extended deadline for Iran to April 6 failed to meaningfully calm market anxieties.

items.headline: Carnival Returns to Profitability, Authorizes Share Buyback
items.summary: Carnival Corporation & plc ($CCL) reported a return to profitability for the quarter ended February 28, 2026, with net income attributable to the company of $258 million, up from a net loss of $78 million a year prior. The company also authorized a $2.5 billion share repurchase program.

items.headline: ZEAL Network SE Beats Earnings Expectations
items.summary: ZEAL Network SE (ETR:TIMA) released its full-year report, with revenues of €220 million, beating expectations by 2.7%. Statutory earnings per share (EPS) of €1.98 were also 12% above analyst forecasts.

items.headline: Disclaimer
items.summary: This digest provides market insights and is not financial advice. Investors should conduct their own due diligence and consult with a financial professional before making any investment decisions.
